normalized_title: Position Statement: Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure
scope_summary: WAPC position statement outlining how electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure should be considered and assessed in the WA planning system. It applies across Western Australia to new proposals and significant redevelopment, guiding location (destinations, transport routes, public buildings, on-street/kerbside), land use, movement-network safety, wayfinding and amenity considerations, and recommends exemptions from development approval for unobtrusive EV charging infrastructure. Table 1 sets recommended minimum provisioning ratios for EV charging by land use, including residential dwellings.

key_numeric_standards.value: Level 1 to each parking bay assigned to a dwelling
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: single house, grouped dwelling, multiple dwelling, single bedroom dwelling, aged and dependents dwelling
car park land use where >50 bays

key_numeric_standards.topic: EV charging provision - freeway service centre
EV charging provision - hotel/motel guest parking
key_numeric_standards.value: Level 1 to each guest bay; Level 2 to 2% of communal bays (min 1)
Level 1 - one bay per powered site; Level 1 - 50% of communal bays

key_numeric_standards.topic: EV charging provision - cinema/theatre, civic use, exhibition centre, private recreation
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: cinema/theatre >200 people; civic use >1,000m2; exhibition centre and recreation-private >1,500m2 floorspace

definitions: Facilities for electric vehicle charging equipment

cross_references.cited: National Construction Code (NCC), including Part J9
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
